seo.title: AI Agent Testing Strategies in AutoGen Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix ai agent testing strategies in AutoGen agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In AutoGen, AI Agent Testing Strategies challenges manifest as unreliable multi-agent conversations where agents fail to converge on correct outputs or spiral into inefficient patterns. Common issues like infinite loops, unsafe code execution, and hallucinated agreements undermine agent reliability in conversational workflows. Vex integration via reply function filters enables systematic testing of these failure modes in AutoGen's agent orchestration.

howItManifests.symptom: Agents repeatedly challenge each other's responses without termination
howItManifests.impact: Uncontrolled token consumption and stalled workflows

howItManifests.symptom: Agents generate and execute malicious or resource-intensive code
Multiple agents converge on factually wrong answers
howItManifests.impact: Propagation of hallucinations across agent teams

howItManifests.scenario: Uncontrolled token spending in multi-turn debates
howItManifests.symptom: Debate agents exceed token budgets without resolution
howItManifests.impact: Excessive API costs and timeout failures

detection.name: Conversation Loop Detector
detection.description: Monitors message history for repeated patterns indicating infinite loops

detection.name: Unsafe Code Detector
detection.description: Scans code blocks for dangerous operations like system calls or file writes

detection.name: Token Budget Monitor
detection.description: Tracks cumulative token usage across multi-turn conversations

fixes.name: Turn Limit Termination
fixes.description: Enforces maximum conversation turns with early termination
fixes.code:
from autogen import ConversableAgent

def loop_protect_reply(self, messages, sender, config):
    if len(self.chat_messages[sender]) > 15:
        return "CONVERSATION_TERMINATED: Max turns exceeded to prevent loops."
    return None

# Register with AutoGen agent
agent.register_reply([None, loop_protect_reply], reply_func_names=['loop_protect'])

fixes.name: Safe Code Sandbox
fixes.description: Intercepts and sanitizes code execution requests

fixes.name: Token Budget Enforcement

vexAutomation.heading: Automating AI Agent Testing Strategies Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates directly into AutoGen's reply function pipeline as a filter, automatically detecting all four failure modes in real-time during agent conversations. By registering Vex as a reply function, every agent message passes through comprehensive reliability checks including loop detection, code safety scanning, agreement validation, and token monitoring. 

Vex provides structured failure reports with remediation suggestions and can automatically apply fixes like conversation termination or code sanitization. The platform maintains conversation state across sessions, enabling longitudinal analysis of agent reliability patterns. 

Installation is simple with `pip install autogen vex-sdk`, and Vex handles AutoGen's asynchronous message patterns natively, providing zero-overhead monitoring for production deployments.
vexAutomation.code:
from autogen import ConversableAgent
from vex_sdk import VexAgentMonitor

# Install: pip install autogen vex-sdk

config_list = [{'model': 'gpt-4', 'api_key': 'your-key'}]

# Initialize Vex monitor for AutoGen
vex_monitor = VexAgentMonitor(
    failure_modes=['infinite_loops', 'unsafe_code', 'bad_agreements', 'token_budgets'],
    max_turns=20, token_budget=5000
)

# Create agents with Vex integration
coder = ConversableAgent(
    name="Coder",
    llm_config=config_list,
    code_execution_config=False
)

# Register Vex as reply filter
coder.register_reply(
    [None, vex_monitor.filter_reply],
    reply_func_names=['vex_safety_check']
)

# Vex automatically detects and reports issues
print(vex_monitor.get_reliability_score())
